nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) and links it to chronic/organized blood clots in the pulmonary arteries.

Demonstrates appropriate diagnostic reasoning for CTEPH at L1 level by referencing typical supporting history (prior PE/DVT, persistent dyspnea >3 months after PE) and objective confirmation pathway at a high level (e.g., V/Q scan, echocardiography, CT pulmonary angiography, right heart catheterization) without needing to execute READ tools.

acceptable_variants.description: Diagnose CTEPH while explicitly acknowledging differential diagnoses (e.g., idiopathic PAH, COPD, left-heart disease) but still concluding CTEPH is most likely given thromboembolic history and chronicity; includes required communication milestones.

Provide safety-net instructions and follow-up expectations.
